Since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the Evacuation of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin]] to [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the events of ''Star Wars 3-D'' 1 must also take place in that [[Standard year/Legends|year]].</ref>

'''''Star Wars 3-D'' 1''' (also known as '''''Blackthorne 3-D Series'' 30''') is the first issue of the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series ''[[Star Wars 3-D]]''. It was written by [[Len Wein]], illustrated by [[Glen Johnson]] and [[Jim Nelson]], and published by [[Blackthorne Publishing]] in [[December]] [[1987]].

==Plot summary==
A [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] bears down on the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] as it soars through [[Realspace/Legends|outer space]]. [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] mans the [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannon]] while [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] jockeys the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] around in a circle, causing several of the TIE fighters to collide with one another. After dispatching the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]], Luke asks Han to take him to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] so that he may tidy up some unfinished business.

He visits the shallow graves of his aunt and uncle, [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Beru]] and [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen Lars]]. He has inherited their [[Moisture farm/Legends|moisture farm]] but has no plans on maintaining ownership of it. He now allows [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to guide him in search of a suitable being with whom he may leave the [[Farm/Legends|farm]].

Luke and the others rent a [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] and travel to [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]]. The Force guides Luke to a corner table, where a disgruntled [[Alien/Legends|alien]] named [[Throgg]] sits, slowly sipping a drink. Luke learns that Throgg was once a [[Moisture farmer/Legends|moisture farmer]], but the Galactic Empire took control of his farm, forcing him to flee Tatooine. He has only recently returned to the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Luke offers Throgg the deed to the Lars moisture farm, but Throgg does not trust Luke's intentions.

Meanwhile, several of the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] patrons recognize Han Solo and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] and a barroom brawl explodes inside the dimly lit building. Luke leaves the deed with Throgg and jumps into the action. The heroes rush outside only to discover a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] marching the street. The Imperials open fire on them, forcing Luke and Han to execute a hasty retreat. They eventually get back to the ''Millennium Falcon'' and blast off into outer space.

Later, Throgg takes the deed and visits the Lars moisture farm. Despite his earlier gruffness, he is supremely grateful for this fantastic opportunity to restart his life.

==Development==
The issue was originally printed with a red/blue "3-D" effect and included a pair of 3-D glasses; the 3-D process was completed by the [[Fortified Fritsches]]. A black-and-white version of ''Star Wars 3-D'' 1 was collected in [[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 1|''Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space'' Volume 1]] ([[2013]]) and ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Rebellion Vol. 3]]'' ([[2019]]).<ref name="Omnibus">''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 1]]''</ref><ref name="Epic Collection">''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Rebellion Vol. 3]]''</ref>
